title: Dentaria pachystigma
taxonomy: Dentaria pachystigma is classified within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This plant belongs to the genus Dentaria, a group of flowering plants native to eastern North America. The species Dentaria pachystigma is further classified as a perennial plant, characterized by its hairy leaves and white or purple flowers. It is a member of the order Brassicales, which comprises plants with similar morphological characteristics. Dentaria pachystigma is also known as hairy toothwort in some regions, reflecting its unique physical attributes.

intro: Dentaria pachystigma, also known as Pachystigma dentaria, is a species of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dentaria pachystigma include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
